description: Week 6 video of me trying out the platform. This was the very first stage where I managed to get my software to interface and control the platform.
Week 6 video of me shortly after filming the first one. IN this one I just managed to get control the platform USING the virtual plane from X-Plane 11.
This video shows initial muscle calibration testing. I think this was around week 4 or 5 because I was trying to figure out the load outlier on the chart.
Full shot of where I was working at most of the time. This was the university's basement where they keep all their cool toys.
Picture of the muscle calibration rig. The whole thing is held together with hopes and prayers yet it's still standing strong to this day.
Load cell attached to the top of the rig. This was used to measure the muscle's load at any given moment and ensure it remained consistent.
In this video I show how I managed to get all the pressure to distance values for all 4 loads tested on the rig.
Picture of sliding rail below calibration rig. On the left you can see a linear encoder which was used to measure the muscle's contraction at each pressure step.
Calibration data obtained AFTER fixing the jolting issue from a previous blog.
Week 9 video I took. This marked the final week where I would be gathering muscle data and move on with the project.
Me further explaining the final muscle calibration process.
Picture of the MDX rollercoaster ride. Whilst this was not the final wheelchair accessible platform my project used, it was a proof of concept that served it's function perfectly at this stage.
Week 10 progress where I finally implement my calibration data onto the actual platform.
Me manually controlling the platform using a dummy telemetry app.

This is a final validation video where I go over the muscle calibration data collected throughout the project. In essence I check that the pressure values from the data actually result in the contraction distance I expect.
Uncut video of my final year project presentation.

Picture of me celebrating my final day at uni at Mcdonnalds. The attire I am wearing was a promotional realistic Bee crown used in the minecraft movie promotion. I watched that movie in theathers later on. It was a fun time. Chicken Jockey.
Short video of the motion platform moving around in reaction to a demo flight on X-Plane 11.

Picture of Crusty's Flight Simulator. This was a game I made a while ago well before the final year project. I've always had a big interest in game development as practical yet fun problem solving challenges (Stay tuned for posts about that).

A picture I took with some colleagues on the final day in the basement where I worked on my project. The man on the right is Carlos, who was studying for a master’s in robotics, and the one in the middle is Arun, my classmate and WorldSkills Automation teammate.
Picture of me talking with Omer another fellow classmate.
Instagram reel I made for funsies. I was rendering the presentation with captions and I noticed how I looked when sped up. The opportunity was too funny to not pass up.
